business_goal: Allow registered users to securely access their account.
scope: Implementing the login functionality using email and password.
Validating user credentials against stored records.
Generating and returning an authentication token upon successful login.
out_of_scope: Social media login options (e.g., Google, Facebook).

functional_requirements.description: The system must allow users to log in using a registered email address and password.
The system must validate the provided credentials against the user database.
Upon successful login, the system must generate and return a JSON Web Token (JWT) containing the user's authentication details and role.
The system must display a clear, non-specific error message if the provided credentials are invalid.

non_functional_requirements.description: The login API response time must be fast, ideally under 500ms, even under moderate load.
The login form and associated components must be fully responsive across desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.
All error messages must be clear, user-friendly, and guide the user toward resolution without revealing sensitive system information.

user_stories.goal: I want to log in using my email and password
user_stories.benefit: so that I can access my personalized account features on the E-commerce Platform.

user_stories.goal: I want to log in using my credentials
user_stories.benefit: so that I can manage the platform's backend resources.

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid credentials (email and password), when the user submits the login form, the system successfully authenticates the user and returns a JWT token.
Given invalid credentials (e.g., wrong password or non-existent email), when the user submits the login form, the system displays a generic error message and does not return a token.

input_requirements.validation: Must be a valid email format.
Must meet minimum complexity requirements (e.g., 8 characters).

output_requirements.description: A secure, time-limited token used for subsequent API calls.
The role of the logged-in user (e.g., Customer, Admin).

ui_expectations: A modern, clean login form layout.
Dedicated input fields for Email and Password.
A visible loading state indicator during API submission.
A clear, actionable error message display area.

api_expectations.description: Accepts email and password and returns JWT token and user role upon success.

validation_rules.description: Email must match standard RFC 5322 format.
The combination of email and password must match an active user record in the database.

constraints: Must use the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js).
Authentication must be secured using JWT (JSON Web Tokens).
The application must adhere to the MVC architectural pattern.
assumptions: The user account already exists in the database before login attempt.
Passwords are stored securely using a strong hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt).
The system has access to a centralized authentication service or database connection.

risks.mitigation: Implement rate limiting and CAPTCHA on the login endpoint.

dependencies: User Registration Feature (for initial data creation)
